title: STRLGHT - Ring
file_path: Songs/STRLGHT - Ring - Epidemic Electronic.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/STRLGHT - Ring - Epidemic Electronic.jpg
description:
🎵 Listen to our Electronic music playlist: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLl5EMp8HWjV58Fe7o-DVwjTBFyyx-UZ2w
🔥 Subscribe to be part of our community: https://www.youtube.com/c/EpidemicElectronic?sub_confirmation=1

There are few producers who make sounds as unique as STRLGHT. Born and raised in Stockholm, Sweden STRLGHT is an enigmatic electronic music project unlike others. Anthemic and genre-fluid, the music released under STRLGHT is energetic and melodic. Melancholy harmonies shimer through synths as heavy drums pound listeners chests. Living in Paris for high school, STRLGHT was introduced to electronic production by a friend, where it would eventually take over his life. Now, his music has garnered over 20 million streams on Spotify as his musical success mounts. After composing the official Big Brother 2020 song, it was right back to the studio to prepare for the next big release.

title: Mercy - Badshah Feat. Lauren Gottlieb | Official Music Video | Latest Hit Song 2017

thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Mercy - Badshah Feat. Lauren Gottlieb  Official Music Video  Latest Hit Song 2017 - Sony Music India.jpg
description:
👉 Listen & Download "Mercy" : https://smi.lnk.to/Mercy

BADSHAH, the #1 Hit Machine is back with a bang with a sensational new song  - ‘MERCY’ from his forthcoming album O.N.E. The song composed and sung by him has the gorgeous and super talented Lauren Gottlieb grooving to it.  

👉 Subscribe To Sony Music India - http://bit.ly/SonyMusic_YouTube

From being the first song to be shot in the O2 Arena in London to the grandeur getting further accentuated by a Boeing in the backdrop, ‘MERCY’ is an ear worm and has a cool vibe which is quintessential Badshah. The beautiful Lauren Gotllieb is seen swaying on Badshah’s beats and together they look nothing less than smashing!  The music video has been shot by Ben Peters. Let the Party begin !

Enjoy the video with sub-titles in 13 languages! (English, Hindi, Turkish, Arabic, Spanish, German, Bahasa, Malay, Tamil, Telegu, Kannada, Bhojpuri & Gujarati) 

#mercy #badshah #adityadev #laurengottlieb #dancesongs #popsongs 

Song – Mercy
Starring – Badshah & Lauren Gottlieb
Album – ONE (Original Never Ends)
Artist – Badshah
Lyrics / Music  – Badshah
Mix - Badshah & Aditya Dev 
Master – Eric Pillai (Future Sound Of Bombay)
Video Director – Ben Peters
Production - Lavinia Noel
Location - O2 Arena , London
Production Company - Sugarcane Media
Executive Producers -Indiana Mookram & Lavinia Noel
Online Promotion Strategies - Lovish Kathuria 
Music Label - Sony Music India.

Lyrics:
Ik kone mein tha baitha
Jab mujhe woh dikhi thi
Woh sab se alag thi
Woh sab se haseen
Aankhen meri phir uski aankho se ja mili
Ik wari do wari phir nikli hasi
Ik hath hawa vich duje 'ch glass
Te mainu ni pata si glass 'ch ki
But hauli-hauli lak nu ghumake nachdi
Lage so sexy
O takdi mainu main takda ohnu
Mahaul mein tension badhne lagi
Khuda ki kasam na kabhi maine pi
Haan phir bhi thodi-thodi chadne lagi
Ruka na gaya main gaya ohde kol
I said I wanna talk
O kehndi mainu bol

Synopsis:
The tale begins in Mumbai where a sophisticated gang of robbers is sweeping through the city, giving nightmares to the police department. They come like wind, sweep the place and disappear on their hi-tech bikes - the slickest and fastest riding machines on the road.

Officer Jai Dixit (Abhishek Bachchan) is brought in on the case. Soon he is on the trail of the robbers - a gang headed by Kabir (John Abraham), an evil perfectionist with an attitude. Jai ropes in the services of Ali (Uday Chopra), a happy-go-lucky garage mechanic and a prodigious bike rider. The two reluctant partners join forces, and soon Jai realizes that the clown can ride bikes like the wind. Kabir soon catches on to Jai-Ali’s teaming up and his arrogance pushes him to take up the challenge openly. From the mean streets of Mumbai, the chase shifts to the sun kissed beaches of Goa. And so starts the hunt... where sometimes the hunter becomes the hunted.

Dhoom reinvents the classic cops and robbers tale and brings it into the 21st century. With fast bikes, big action, non-stop fun and a thrilling story that leaves you out of breath, Dhoom is a powerhouse of adventure and excitement waiting to explode. It’s like your favourite roller coaster ride... once you’re on, the only thing you want to do is to go back and take that ride all over again!

description:
🎵 Listen to our Electronic music playlist: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLl5EMp8HWjV58Fe7o-DVwjTBFyyx-UZ2w
🔥 Subscribe to be part of our community: https://www.youtube.com/c/EpidemicElectronic?sub_confirmation=1

There are few producers who make sounds as unique as STRLGHT. Born and raised in Stockholm, Sweden STRLGHT is an enigmatic electronic music project unlike others. Anthemic and genre-fluid, the music released under STRLGHT is energetic and melodic. Melancholy harmonies shimer through synths as heavy drums pound listeners chests. Living in Paris for high school, STRLGHT was introduced to electronic production by a friend, where it would eventually take over his life. Now, his music has garnered over 20 million streams on Spotify as his musical success mounts. After composing the official Big Brother 2020 song, it was right back to the studio to prepare for the next big release.

Coming out of Compton, California Roddy Ricch combines introspective rhymes and melodic trap. Influenced by various artists and styles, Roddy Ricch began recording at 16, releasing his debut mixtape in 2017. Roddy Ricch would break through in 2018 with the single "Die Young,'' and top the Billboard 200 with his debut album 'Please Excuse Me for Being Antisocial' in 2019, which included the hit song "The Box." In 2020, Roddy Ricch would follow up on the success of 'Please Excuse Me for Being Antisocial' with a Grammy win for Best Rap Performance as a feature on Nipsey Hussle's "Racks In the Middle" along with fellow LA producer Hit-Boy. In 2024, Roddy is preparing to release his third studio album 'The Navy Album'.

title: Petit Biscuit - Suffer ft. SKOTT (Music Video)
file_path: Songs/Petit Biscuit - Suffer ft. SKOTT (Music Video) - Petit Biscuit.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Petit Biscuit - Suffer ft. SKOTT (Music Video) - Petit Biscuit.jpg
description:
Petit Biscuit - Suffer ft. SKOTT (Official Video)
Make sure to subscribe to Petit Biscuit and turn on notifications to stay updated with all new uploads! http://petitbiscu.it/youtubesubscribe 


Petit Biscuit: 
“We asked Theo & Akim, the two directors, to write a story that was about fire, with a darker atmosphere. I love how they turned this into a weird love story, with all those flashbacks that the girl has, of what was once good then turned bad. It’s telling us well about how our memories are important to build ourselves, even if it hurts a lot remembering them.”

SKOTT
"I was excited to write on the track when Petit Biscuit sent it over, it's full of wild energy. The lyrics are about the two sides of loneliness – on one hand it can be isolating, but on the other it can be liberating or empowering to be on your own. And I feel like the video kind of leaves it open to interpretation. While the burning girl is symbolism for pain and suffering, she also comes out of it alive and unharmed, with almost a sense of determination in her walk. Like she's gone through some stuff and come out stronger."

Directors: Rosalia Kin
Production: HK Corp
Actors: Garance Marillier & Sandor Funtek

thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Skee-Lo - I Wish (Official Video) - Altra Moda Music.jpg
description:
Download & Stream: https://AltraModaMusic.lnk.to/rWJhN

"I Wish" is a hip-hop song by rapper Skee-Lo released in 1995 as the first single from his debut album of the same name. The track peaked at number thirteen on the Billboard Hot 100, number fifteen in the United Kingdom and number four in Sweden and Norway.The song was nominated for Best Rap Solo Performance at the 1996 Grammy Awards.

In the song Skee-Lo is wishing for a girl, but she says he is not her type. He wishes he was more like her boyfriend, taller and a basketball player (baller). He also wishes for a better car, specifically a '64 Impala.

"I Wish" is heard in the 2006 film, American Pie Presents: The Naked Mile.
"I Wish" is also featured in the 2013 Super Bowl add of the Toyota RAV4.

Synopsis:
In a world of labels it’s easy to forget who made sportsmen, pop singers and matinee idols into stars, superstars and megastars - the FANS. Every FAN has their own personal relationship with their hero - it’s almost a Love Story.

FAN is the story of Gaurav (Shah Rukh Khan) a young man, 20 something, whose world revolves around the mega movie star Aryan Khanna (Shah Rukh Khan) or God as he refers to him.

From the by lanes of Delhi, young Gaurav embarks on a journey to the city of dreams, Mumbai, in order to wish his God on his birthday. After all, he is Aryan’s biggest FAN and even has a striking resemblance to him - how difficult could it be for the world biggest FAN to get an audience with the world’s biggest Star.

When things don't go according to plan, Gaurav’s love and passion for his God turns in to a dangerous obsession that crosses the fine line.

In an edge of the seat thriller, FAN will peel away at both Gaurav and Aryan’s personalities and characters as the two men discover people within themselves that even they didn't know existed, and we’re left wondering who to sympathize with and whom to root for - can we really pick a side, after all, Superstars are also human beings but each one of us is also a FAN.

title: Pulp Fiction Misirlou
file_path: Songs/Pulp Fiction Misirlou - Music & Movie Moments – Christiaan Janssens.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Pulp Fiction Misirlou - Music & Movie Moments – Christiaan Janssens.jpg
description:
The story of "Misirlou" is a compelling chronicle of a melody in perpetual motion. Originating in the Eastern Mediterranean, this traditional song has been a vibrant traveler for over a century, seamlessly adapting to the cultures, languages, and musical styles it has encountered. Its very name, "Misirlou," meaning "Egyptian Girl," is a linguistic artifact of its journey, borrowed from the Turkish "Mısırlı" (Egyptian) and adapted into Greek.

The song first flourished in the cosmopolitan milieu of the late Ottoman Empire, a region where Greek, Turkish, Arabic, Armenian, and Jewish communities lived in close proximity. In this vibrant, multi-ethnic environment, "Misirlou" was not owned by any single group but was a shared treasure in the collective repertoire of musicians. Its transition from oral tradition to recorded history began in the early 20th century. Among the first commercial recordings was the 1927 version by Greek singer Tetos Demetriades in the United States. Shortly after, in Greece, Michalis Patrinos cemented its place in the rebetiko canon with a slower, more haunting version that used the tsifteteli belly dance rhythm. The lyrics of this version, which narrated a risky love affair between a Greek Christian and an Egyptian Muslim, added a layer of social transgression, making the song feel daring and modern.

The melody's voyage continued across the Atlantic, where it was adopted by Greek and Middle Eastern diaspora communities in American cafés and clubs. A significant, and somewhat controversial, milestone came in 1941 when Greek-American musician Nick Roubanis published a jazz arrangement and successfully copyrighted it as his own composition, a bold move given the tune's well-established folk status. This act facilitated its entry into the American mainstream, where big band leaders like Harry James and Woody Herman crafted swing and exotic lounge versions. During this period, "Misirlou" became a staple for belly dance performances and was heavily used to cultivate a Western fantasy of the "Orient," or what was often termed "exotica" music.

Perhaps the most radical reinterpretation arrived in 1962 from Dick Dale, the "King of the Surf Guitar." Drawing on the Arabic melodies he heard from his Lebanese immigrant father, Dale reimagined "Misirlou" as a blistering surf rock instrumental. His use of rapid-fire tremolo picking on a single string, drenched in powerful reverb, transformed the ancient melody into a youthful, high-voltage showpiece. Decades later, Quentin Tarantino's use of Dale's version in Pulp Fiction (1994) catapulted the song to a new peak of global fame, permanently linking it with the film's iconic, gritty coolness. It has since appeared in countless other media, from video games to commercials, becoming a true cultural shorthand.

Musically, the song's unique character comes from its foundation in the double harmonic minor scale (known in Arabic music as maqam Hijaz). This scale, with its distinctive intervals of a semitone followed by an augmented second, is responsible for the melody's instantly recognizable "Eastern" flavor. While this has led to its use as a convenient atmospheric cue in Western media, its true history is far richer: it is a product of cultural fusion and continuous reinvention.

Every new rendition—from the soulful rebetiko of the past to modern electric, jazz, or even electronic arrangements—adds another layer to its enduring legacy. The practice of preserving the core melody while freely altering its rhythm, instrumentation, and context is itself a tribute to the song's living, evolving nature. "Misirlou" is not a museum piece to be preserved under glass, but a dynamic musical force, still capable of generating new energy, new grooves, and new meanings for listeners today.

"Any of you fucking pricks move, and i’ll execute every motherfucking last one of ya" (Honey Bunny in Pulp Fiction)

title: Hardwell & Headhunterz feat. Haris - Nothing Can Hold Us Down (OUT NOW!) #UnitedWeAre
file_path: Songs/Hardwell & Headhunterz feat. Haris - Nothing Can Hold Us Down (OUT NOW!) #UnitedWeAre - Hardwell.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Hardwell & Headhunterz feat. Haris - Nothing Can Hold Us Down (OUT NOW!) #UnitedWeAre - Hardwell.jpg
description:
Hardwell & Headhunterz feat. Haris - Nothing Can Hold Us Down 
OUT NOW! http://bit.ly/UNITEDWEARE
Sign-Up to WIN one of the signed CD's: http://unitedweare.djhardwell.com/

Hardwell: 
I’ve always been a big fan of hardstyle as a genre and it has been huge in Holland for the past 10 years. So I jumped into the studio with Headhunterz, who has always been on my list to work with especially after he delivered a huge remix for ‘Spaceman.’

While working with the amazing vocal by Dutch talent Haris, we wanted to combine both Willem’s harder style with my progressive sound. What we both have in common is that our melodies are the lead in our productions and we used that a starting point. 

At the end, we built in a short variation on the track that it could have sounded like as well.
-----
After a year that saw him retain his crown of World #1 DJ for a second consecutive year, sweep and dominate the festival landscape with colossal peak time performances everywhere from Future Music Festival in Australia to Tomorrowland’s 10th anniversary edition in Belgium, take his explosive party concept I AM HARDWELL on a globe-trotting run of 6 continents across of 32 dates that cumulated with a sold-out show at Madison Square Garden, as well as front his own (and first ever) residency in Ibiza at Ushuaïa with his globally energized I AM HARDWELL concept, alongside scoring numerous chart and anthemic hit releases in tracks such as ‘Dare You’, ‘Everybody Is In The Place’, and his remix of Coldplay’s ‘A Sky Full of Stars’, Hardwell’s entry into 2015 has been met with the arrival of United We Are – his much awaited debut album.

Receiving its worldwide release 23rd January 2015, United We Are, is available for pre-order now via iTunes. Showcasing an impressive line-up of collaborations that Jason Derulo, Fatman Scoop, W&W, Bright Lights, Harrison, Jonathan Mendelsohn, Amba Shepherd, Joey Dale, Luciana Headhunterz, Chris Jones, Haris, Mr Probz, Funkerman feat. I-fan and DallasK, as well as a track together with Tiësto and singer-songwriter Andreas Moe, United We Are is an album primed with club ready hits that will no doubt become the soundtrack to the summer festival season.

To coincide with the launch of the album on January 23rd and 24th, Hardwell kicks off his second world tour I AM HARDWELL - United We Are at the Ziggodome in Amsterdam. The concert on January 23rd is accessible for all ages; both concerts are sold out.

Full album tracklist #UnitedWeAre :

title: Ooyy - Kenji
file_path: Songs/Ooyy - Kenji - Epidemic Electronic.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Ooyy - Kenji - Epidemic Electronic.jpg
description:
🎵 Listen to our Electronic music playlist: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLl5EMp8HWjV58Fe7o-DVwjTBFyyx-UZ2w
🔥 Subscribe to be part of our community: https://www.youtube.com/c/EpidemicElectronic?sub_confirmation=1

You might not know who Ooyy is, but you’ve probably heard his music. Hailing from Stockholm, Sweden and producing music under the moniker Ooyy, Henrik Olsson is currently one of the most synchronised artists worldwide, both online and offline.

His unique sound incorporates a blend of future bass, electronic hooks and organic elements. Frequently using sound effects as instruments, the immense detail within his sound creates captivating dynamics rich with builds and tension. A feeling of cinematic storytelling is at the heart of his soundscapes, and it is this that has made him such a hit with content creators worldwide. While Henrik’s musical influences are constantly evolving, this soundscaping stays at the core of his music, whether industrial or natural. Crossing genre boundaries, introducing vocals to his productions, and incorporating new sounds and instruments have been important developments within his music as a way to tell new stories.

As well as releasing his debut album Kopfkino in 2021, he’s soundtracked campaigns for Daniel Wellington, the H&M collaboration with the Swedish Olympics squad for the 2018 Winter Games, and has been the subject of a documentary by the iconic YouTuber and photographer Peter McKinnon. Being a part of the creator community himself, Henrik wants to inspire people to imagine their own stories and cinematics to his songs. Hear one Ooyy song, and you’ll see what he means.

Follow Ooyy: http://link.epidemicsound.com/OOOYYY

description:
Download/Stream: https://maddecent.ffm.to/harlemshake

The official Mad Decent YouTube release of Baauer - Harlem Shake. Stream the full track and other Baauer releases here or show support on iTunes or Google Play.

Mixed and Mastered by Curt Cameruci of Flosstradamus

The love child of Brooklyn and the Internet, Baauer was born out of noise. A sound that began steeped in the 4/4 dance tradition, slowly took on hip hop tendencies until the two became one. Influenced heavily by southern hip-hop and UK bass sounds, Baauer has found himself smack dab in the middle of the electronic renaissance. He is the delectable fusion of future-crunk and leather shaken aggressively in a bottle for years and has only just opened the top to pour the mixture out.

Harlem Shake his been featured on almost every Diplo and Friends mix to date including Diplo, Brodinski, and Flosstradamus as well as being featured in Rustie's Lastest Essential mix!

Visit http://jeffrees.tumblr.com/ for all past Jeffree releases!

Bruno Mars is a 16x GRAMMY®-winning global superstar, singer, songwriter, multi-instrumentalist, producer, and one of the most-streamed artists in the world. Known for his showmanship and chart-toppers, Bruno has set a number of records throughout his career, including becoming the first artist ever to hit 150 million monthly listeners on Spotify, appearing on two of the fastest songs to reach one billion streams (for “Die with a Smile” with Lady Gaga and “APT.” with ROSÉ, and achieving the highest-certified song in RIAA history with 2010’s “Just the Way You Are”). Since launching a string of hits beginning in 2009, Bruno has sold over 150 million records worldwide, making him one of the best-selling artists of all time. Beyond his work as a soloist, Bruno is a member of the duo Silk Sonic with Anderson .Paak. In addition to his 35 Billboard Hot 100 hits, including nine number one singles, Bruno is the recipient of 16 GRAMMY® Awards (including “Album of the Year” for 24K Magic), 14 American Music Awards, and seven MTV Video Music Awards. His 24K Magic World Tour was among the highest-grossing tours in history, and within the top ten highest-grossing of the 2010s.

Synopsis:
Have you ever stopped to think if the most ordinary, uninteresting, unobtrusive man you might see on the road or around you might have a love story to tell? Maybe not! How can an ordinary man have a breathtaking, goose flesh igniting, awe inspiring love story of all things to tell?

This is what happened to Surinder Sahni (Shah Rukh Khan) - a simple, clean hearted, honest man, leading a humdrum life, when he meets his total opposite and finds love in the flamboyant, fun-loving, vivacious - Taani (Anushka Sharma) for whom the whole world is her canvas and she paints her own life with the colours of rainbow all until unforeseen circumstances changes it all and brings them together.

What follows is a journey filled with laughter, tears, joy, pain, music, dance and a lot of love. A journey that makes us believe that there is an extraordinary love story in every ordinary jodi (couple).

Have you ever stopped to think if the most ordinary, uninteresting, unobtrusive man you might see on the road or around you might have a love story to tell? Maybe not! How can an ordinary man have a breathtaking, goose flesh igniting, awe inspiring love story of all things to tell?

This is what happened to Surinder Sahni (Shah Rukh Khan) - a simple, clean hearted, honest man, leading a humdrum life, when he meets his total opposite and finds love in the flamboyant, fun-loving, vivacious - Taani (Anushka Sharma) for whom the whole world is her canvas and she paints her own life with the colours of rainbow all until unforeseen circumstances changes it all and brings them together.

What follows is a journey filled with laughter, tears, joy, pain, music, dance and a lot of love. A journey that makes us believe that there is an extraordinary love story in every ordinary jodi (couple).

Dylan Sitts is a producer based in Brooklyn. A versatile musician with a deep bag of tricks, he seamlessly blends hip hop with other genres, creating clever musical fusions that defy easy categorization. Taking cues from the legendary rap producers of the ‘90s and early ‘00s, he specializes in breathing new life into old and often obscure samples. Whether he’s producing towering, anthemic instrumentals or laidback beats that inspire reflection, he creates music with finesse and curiosity. Sitts has earned production credits with Young M.A, Everyone You Know, and HDBeenDope, and his music has been placed in ads by Volvo, Vogue, and Greenpeace and shows on Netflix and HBO Max. He has amassed over 160 million streams to date.

Sultan is a story of Sultan Ali Khan - a local wrestling champion with the world at his feet as he dreams of representing India at the Olympics.

It’s a story of Aarfa - a feisty young girl from the same small town as Sultan with her own set of dreams.

When the 2 local wrestling legends lock horns, romance blossoms and their dreams and aspirations become intertwined and aligned.

However, the path to glory is a rocky one and one must fall several times before one stands victorious - More often than not, this journey can take a lifetime. Sultan is a classic underdog tale about a wrestler's journey, looking for a comeback by defeating all odds staked up against him. But when he has nothing to lose and everything to gain in this fight for his life match… Sultan must literally fight for his life.

Sultan believes he’s got what it takes… but this time, it’s going to take everything he’s got.

title: Exyl - Ping! 2
file_path: Songs/Exyl - Ping! 2 - Exyl.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Exyl - Ping! 2 - Exyl.jpg
description:
IM BACK BABY. I'VE LEVELED UP.
Listen everywhere: https://ffm.to/exyl-ping-2
Let's connect: https://ffm.bio/exyl

I have spent an absolutely insane amount of time on this. You don't understand. I started the project on 4/5/2020 and it's been 663 days. I'd wake up every day contemplating the monumental task of finishing this project, and today is finally the day where I have completed it. It's truly my best piece of work and will be, for a long time hahah.

Back in the original "Ping!", the flute part was just goofy text spelling out "F L U T E"... I've come a long way since, as this music video is a culmination of my progress in music & animation.

I wanted to create this cyber-themed masterpiece as a sequel to the beloved “Ping!” remix of Discord’s notification sounds But funny enough, the project file itself comes from the remains of an Undertale remix I scrapped (of the song Megalovania)... The song AND video are so insanely detailed that the project files are almost impossible to open without crashing LOL.. But it was soooo worth it.

The beatdrop is super hard-hitting. The bass is a really cool sound design experiment made from an acid Serum wavetable with heavy distortion & filter automations. An absurd amount of hours were spent meticulously tweaking the sound design and mixing. Tons of cinematic elements woven in, especially later in the song, not to mention the beloved flute part. This song reached over 5 million+ views on YouTube, and it set a whole new standard for me as a creative. Love yall.

...

Lyrics:

You better fight,  
You better fight for me  

Go.

Fight for me (x2)

(legendary flute riff)
  
You better fight... (corrupted)

...

Epilepsy warning. 
Song ID for GD creators: 1107630

#exyl #discord #electro

This is game music featured in Geometry Dash, Roblox / Epic Minigames, Blockland and other titles.  For more, check out the playlist here: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL5xceeTDjuOPm0Uy9ihv1y7jT4cjPGRe-
---
This is my music submission for the NGADM Round 4!
I spent all day making this, pretty much from start to end, as this was the only day this week i had time to make a song!

I couldn't decide on what style or genre i wanted to make, and after 2 failed attempts, this came out. a hybrid of.. pretty much everything, a damn clutter.
Or if you were to try and put it in a genre: Glitch-step-Orchestral-break-Techno... lol.
I personally like it a bit chaotic and surprising, tho. so I'm happy with it! it was really fun to make in any case! ^^
And to all of you asking me to do dubstep, there you go, I guess.

file_path: Songs/IMPOSSIBLE - Ballistic Wistfully by Eightos - fnm04.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/IMPOSSIBLE - Ballistic Wistfully by Eightos - fnm04.jpg
description:
Macro by: 
Prototype Hipo (0-55, 90-100) 
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCnJhTBtnUI9siOSmh0GBSyQ
Melting Ice (55-90)
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC42ui8-W54enZYD2cHrLqXA

This is the NEW version of Ballistic Wistfully built to comply with ILL rules. "Doesn't meet quality rules at 30-41, 66-90".Therefore this is the fixed version featuring some major (mirror portal to differentiate between versions) and minor (actual gameplay) fixes. Adventure of a goofy egg on its quest for life answers. The style of this song is sillier than my usual banger beatdrops, so it's understandable that it didn't get as much views, but I hope you enjoy it nonetheless. It's whimsy and cartoon-y. description:

- Glorious Morning 2: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yolbGaJD4AY&list=PLsKWgfWtIc5oBxZAYuP5CXJl9_qG3Awn7&index=8

- Glorious Thunderzone: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Vkqdk6dDbHA

For more game music of this style, check out my Techno-Orchestral music playlist here!
https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLsKWgfWtIc5oBxZAYuP5CXJl9_qG3Awn7

--------------------------------

Made in 2007!

This music has a long history of development. it started out as a small victory fanfare for a game called mecha pumble, but i liked the clip so much after finishing it that I decided to try to make something more of it. the original song is about twice in length,  but pulled out / just has more of the same. later when the contest for castlecrashers songs came up, I cleaned it up into a loop and submitted it, I figured it would go well for something of an intro. I placed 4th, if i recall correctly.
later this music was used as the theme song in a few flash games, like for instance, age of war, siegius, miragine war, etc.
Now a final remake is in production, but until then, enjoy this, almost classic tune by now ;)

Most my music is royalty-free as background music depending on use, read here:

Bruno Mars is a 16x GRAMMY®-winning global superstar, singer, songwriter, multi-instrumentalist, producer, and one of the most-streamed artists in the world. Known for his showmanship and chart-toppers, Bruno has set a number of records throughout his career, including becoming the first artist ever to hit 150 million monthly listeners on Spotify, appearing on two of the fastest songs to reach one billion streams (for “Die with a Smile” with Lady Gaga and “APT.” with ROSÉ, and achieving the highest-certified song in RIAA history with 2010’s “Just the Way You Are”). Since launching a string of hits beginning in 2009, Bruno has sold over 150 million records worldwide, making him one of the best-selling artists of all time. Beyond his work as a soloist, Bruno is a member of the duo Silk Sonic with Anderson .Paak. In addition to his 35 Billboard Hot 100 hits, including nine number one singles, Bruno is the recipient of 16 GRAMMY® Awards (including “Album of the Year” for 24K Magic), 14 American Music Awards, and seven MTV Video Music Awards. His 24K Magic World Tour was among the highest-grossing tours in history, and within the top ten highest-grossing of the 2010s.

title: Vegeta Super Saiyan Theme [2018 Epic Cinematic Cover]
file_path: Songs/Vegeta Super Saiyan Theme [2018 Epic Cinematic Cover] - DDRMR.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Vegeta Super Saiyan Theme [2018 Epic Cinematic Cover] - DDRMR.jpg
description:
★Hey guys! Please subscribe to my channel as I'm hoping to finally hit 10k so I can share my works in progress with ya'll via YouTube stories! Feel free to comment as well! You are allowed to use this music in your videos so long as you provide credit to the original video and / or my YouTube channel.

★Growing up on the Funimation dub of Dragon Ball Z, Bruce Faulconers soundtrack was a massive reason as to why I fell in love with this series! It's been a lifelong dream to one day remix some of the themes that means so much to me and I finally felt like I had enough production skill to do them justice! Vegeta, arguably the deepest and most developed character in all of Dragon Ball, is a justifiably a fan favourite. When the original Vegeta Super Saiyan theme played during Vegetas first transformation against the Andoids I remember having a rush of shivers going down all over my body, with this effect doubling during his final flash against Cell. I absolutely had to make this my first DBZ remix!

★Credits
►Cover Produced by Boriz Safi (Instagram: @ddrmr69)
►Guitar Performed by Kevin Robb of Red House Productions
►Mixed and Mastered by FWLR (Instagram: @fwlrmusic)
►Video Animation by Boriz Safi
►Majin Vegeta Artwork by 69Ani

My world is finally starting to take shape.

Expect to have the universe by tomorrow.

I'd rather see the whole world go back to black. 

But then I'm blinded by this light from nowhere. 
This blue planet with life and an atmosphere. 

I look closer and I can't believe. 
What I'm seeing down there, is me. 


But if it's me looking down on me. 
The world above must also be. 

This could go on for infinity.

About Dua Lipa:
Born and raised in London to Kosovar-Albanian parents Dua Lipa signed to Warner Records in 2015, releasing her self titled debut in 2017 which featured the hit singles, 'Be The One', 'IDGAF', 'Hotter Than Hell' & her first UK #1 hit 'New Rules'. 

Critical success soon followed as Dua Lipa picked up Brit Awards for 'Best Female Solo Artist' and 'Best British Breakthrough Act' as well as Grammy Awards for 'Best New Artist' and 'Best Dance Recording' for the track 'Electricity' in collaboration with Silk City.

Her second internationally acclaimed studio album 'Future Nostalgia' was released in 2020 and included the hit singles 'Don't Start Now, 'Break My Heart', 'Hallucinate' and 'Levitating', with the remix album 'Club Future Nostalgia' following shortly after featuring guest appearances from 'Blackpink', 'Missy Elliott', 'Madonna' and 'Gwen Stefani'.

title: Roy Jones Jr. - Can't Be Touched (feat Mr. Magic & Trouble)
file_path: Songs/Roy Jones Jr. - Can't Be Touched (feat Mr. Magic & Trouble) - RapClassicsTV.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Roy Jones Jr. - Can't Be Touched (feat Mr. Magic & Trouble) - RapClassicsTV.jpg
description:
Roy Jones Jr's track "Can't be Touched" featuring Mr. Magic and Trouble, from his legendary album "Body Head Bangerz: Volume One."

"Body Head Bangerz: Volume One" is the first album by the famous American boxer turned rapper, Roy Jones Jr., and his group, Body Head Bangerz. It first came out on August 3, 2004, under Body Head Entertainment, but it got re-released on October 26, 2004, by Universal Music with some changes. The new version had a different album cover and added two new songs called "Can't Let Go" and "Getting Money Right," but they took out one song called "Down Here." The clean version of the re-release had the same songs as the original but without any bad words. The album has songs with other famous southern hip hop artists like B.G., Lil' Flip, Petey Pablo, Mike Jones, and Bun B. It had two hit songs, "I Smoke, I Drank" and "Can't Be Touched," which were played a lot on BET and did well on music charts. One even made it onto the Billboard Hot 100 list. Also, one of the album's tracks, "Body Head Anthem," was featured in the popular video game "Midnight Club 3: DUB Edition." This album is remembered as an important part of hip hop history.

Connect with Roy Jones Jr:
 https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCYVdxsrqtUNYEGrtsMCBzOA?feature=gws_kp_artist&feature=gws_kp_artist
https://music.apple.com/us/artist/roy-jones-jr/22585135
https://open.spotify.com/artist/5K6YsAKuRtcczPgxAdzc3B?autoplay=true
https://www.deezer.com/artist/874450

Roy Jones Jr. is a famous American boxer who's considered one of the best fighters ever. He was born on January 16, 1969, in Pensacola, Florida. He started boxing professionally in 1989 and got really famous because he was super quick, athletic, and had a unique way of fighting. During his career, he won lots of championships in different weight classes, from middleweight to heavyweight. People called him "Captain Hook" because of his really strong left punch. Besides boxing, he also made music, like rap albums, and worked with other musicians. Even though he had some tough times later in his career, his impact on boxing is huge, and he's remembered as one of the greatest boxers ever.

Roll with us, be part of the rap culture, and watch us lead the way in the hip hop game. We ain't just a label, we're a family and a community. The movement pushes the boundaries of rap. Stay tuned cause the next big thing in rap music is cookin' right here.

title: O-Zone - Dragostea Din Tei [Official Video]
file_path: Songs/O-Zone - Dragostea Din Tei [Official Video] - Time Records.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/O-Zone - Dragostea Din Tei [Official Video] - Time Records.jpg
description:
O-Zone - Dragostea Din Tei [Official Video]
iTunes: https://itunes.apple.com/it/album/dragostea-din-tei/1295665938
Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/album/5KJRYCxiahHmBjZkzY4HzL

"Dragostea Din Tei" è una della tante hit della dance band moldava O-Zone. La loro storia è cominciata nella Repubblica Moldava, dove Dan, Arsenie e Radu hanno conosciuto i loro primi successi musicali. In questo modo si sono convinti che la musica pop rumena poteva essere interessante x quanto riguarda il mercato europeo e potevano esserci i presupposti per l'inizio di una carriera Internazionale. Ecco perché nel 2002 gli O-Zone hanno dato un loro demo a Dan Popi un manager di un'etichetta discografica rumena nel quale hanno riposto la loro fiducia. Il loro primo singolo in Romania è stato: "Numai Tu" tratto dal loro album "Number 1". Numai Tu ha conquistato le classifiche radio e la Top 100 rumena. Nell'autunno 2002 gli O-zone tornano con un nuovo singolo "Despre Tine" e le nuove stelle della musica dance si accorgono di aver contaminato sia le radio sia le televisioni della Romania con "l'O-Zone Mania".

"Dragostea Din Tei" by O-Zone was one of the most played singles by the Italian radio stations and reached NR 1 in all the European sales charts: Nr 1 in France for 15 weeks (Platinum Record), Nr 1 in Spain, Nr 1 in Germany (Platinum Record), Nr 3 in the UK, Nr 1 in Belgium (Platinum Record), Nr 1 in Holland (Platinum Record), Nr 1 in Norway (Platinum Record in 2 week), Nr 1 in Austria, Switzerland and in all the Eastern European Countries (...)

title: Ping!
file_path: Songs/Ping! - Exyl.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Ping! - Exyl.jpg
description:
Listen everywhere: https://ffm.to/exyl-ping
Let's connect: https://ffm.bio/exyl

Hi flute gang. I wanna tell you more about this song because it changed my life.

Back in 2018 I was just some random kid on Discord, joining the newly started communities of my music producer idols and trying to beg for a collab. My attempts got ghosted, but i often got good feedback on my songs at least… So I kept improving.

barely a few weeks after uploading my 2nd video on this channel, Discord themselves hosted a 4-day contest called Discord Hackweek 2019. I was nearing the end of highschool, and my parents would constantly take away my PC because they wanted me to study for my finals. So I would sneak into their room at night and steal the laptop (sneak 100). I pulled all nighters working on Ping. But then I got caught by my mom halfway through working on ping, and she was gonna tell my dad, but i begged her to wait until i complete the project... So yeah. I completed the project, and then never saw that laptop again. My dad smashed it into pieces. I lost so many projects, it's a bit sad.

Still, I had my phone. i would still check on my phone to see if it won the contest... sadly I didn't. I got like 100 views again. But I got some hope after Discord randomly decided to include me in their blog thanks to Zencha. They didn't do this for any other project. After that shoutout, I got a huge burst of views over the course of 6 months, totaling 10,000... I remember talking to my middle eastern classmates and being all smug about it, flexing on them lol. I thought that was the end of my music career, it was a fun ride.

Except something miraculous happened. On December 22 of 2019 i woke up at midnight because my phone was constantly throwing notifications... i checked my phone and saw that it was getting spammed by youtube comments and discord dm’s... i checked the song on youtube, and it had 100k views overnight. WTF.

That morning i was jumping all over the house from joy... but my parents were NOT thrilled. Had to go back to study. So I did. But I was still constantly on my phone, frantically responding to the flood of discord dm's and checking the comments daily. I panicked because everyone wanted the song to be on spotify. So i found a way to upload it... but due to the Christmas break, that distributor delayed me by 3 months and I was devastated. The algorithm slowed down at around 600k views, and the song was no longer that viral anymore. So I made no money from it. 

I decided to joke about that situation in the subtitles. I ended up getting carried away and making goofy comments all across the entire song. But then another miracle happened. Over the next few weeks, purely because of the subtitles, Ping's viewcount exploded to 10, 20, 30 million... and because now it WAS on all the different streaming services including Spotify, Ping singlehandedly paid the bills and allowed me to have my dream career.

I didn't post consistently because a lot of life stuff happened between then and now... Had to deal with some isolation and health issues. But now I realized that music is... really all I have. I really enjoy improving and I want to keep working on cooler and cooler projects for everyone to enjoy & be inspired by. Love yall :)

...

Epilepsy warning. 
Song ID for GD creators: 900250

Also the BPM is 142.86
LOL. Don't ask.

#exyl #discord

title: Las Ketchup - The Ketchup Song (Asereje) (Spanglish Version) (Official Video)
file_path: Songs/Las Ketchup - The Ketchup Song (Asereje) (Spanglish Version) (Official Video) - Altra Moda Music.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Las Ketchup - The Ketchup Song (Asereje) (Spanglish Version) (Official Video) - Altra Moda Music.jpg
description:
"The Ketchup Song" is the English title of the song "Aserejé," recorded by the Spanish pop group Las Ketchup. It was released in July 2002 as the lead single from their debut album, Hijas del Tomate. 

In the summer of 2002 they had a major flamenco Europop fusion hit with "Aserejé" (released as "The Ketchup Song" in the UK and other countries), which reached the top of the charts in many countries including Spain, Portugal, Colombia, UK, Italy, Greece, France, Finland, Turkey, Syria, Sweden, Norway, Belgium, Netherlands, Philippines, Switzerland, Germany, Romania, and Australia.

Awards:
2002 'Ondas awards'
-- Best New Artist/group
2004 Billboard Latinos awards
-- Best pop album from new generation ("Las hijas del Tomate")
-- Most tropical-salsa song played by Duo or Group ("The Ketchup Song" (Aserejé)
2003 Latin Grammy Award
-- Best Pop Album By a Duo or Group with Vocals Las hijas del Tomate (Nominated, lost to Bacilos Caraluna.)
2003 Record Fair MIDEM (Cannes)
-- Best New Artist/group
2003 Amigo Awards
-- Best New Group
-- Best New Artist
2004 Record fair MIDEM (Cannes)
-- Breakers of borders (Best-selling Album out of its country and inside of Europe with "Las hijas del Tomate") Song)

title: Geometry Dash - Black Blizzard Verified (Live)
file_path: Songs/Geometry Dash - Black Blizzard Verified (Live) - KrmaL.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Geometry Dash - Black Blizzard Verified (Live) - KrmaL.jpg
description:
Here it is, boys.
It feels good to finally have this out. I started working on this level way back in November with the intention of making it not only my hardest, but also my best level yet. That left me with a bit of a dilemma midway, though.
Through the process of verification, there were a lot of rumors and speculations drawn about many aspects of this level that stirred a bit of drama and unnecessarily high expectations for me, due to the fact that this is the first time I've decided to release a really hard level that I also put effort into making. Thus, I want to clear a few things up.

Because it's too long for a YouTube description, you can read my rant here.
https://pastebin.com/t4eNQtXt

With all that said, I'm pretty happy to have this released now. It's really a work for myself more than anything, just to see what I could do, and I hope others can find enjoyment in it. That being said, I ask that if you enjoy this level or its song, you should make sure to check out Creo's other works as well and give him some positive, non-spammy attention. As for my other levels, since I haven't done this enough, do the same. Remember the levels wouldn't exist without the track artists' work that was probably even harder than mine. Other than that, I want this level not to be remembered only for its difficulty, but more so for the quality of it. So, please, tell me how I did. Thank you all, we'll see where we go from here!

channel: The Best Of - Home Of Classic Music
file_path: Songs/Lynyrd Skynyrd - Free Bird - The Best Of - Home Of Classic Music.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Lynyrd Skynyrd - Free Bird - The Best Of - Home Of Classic Music.jpg
description:
Subscribe to The Best Of for more classic music history, videos and playlists: http://bit.ly/WdJ36u

Southern rock legends Lynyrd Skynyrd recorded "Free Bird" in 1973 and it featured on their debut album "(Pronounced 'lĕh-'nérd 'skin-'nérd)".

Released as a single in November 1974, "Free Bird" became the band's second Top 40 hit on the Billboard Hot 100 chart in early 1975, where it peaked at No. 19. A live version of the song also reached the Billboard Hot 100 chart in 1977, peaking at No. 38.

Special thanks to James at the Cowboymod Emporium for his help in the making of this video.

title: Rod Stewart - Young Turks (Official HD Remaster)
file_path: Songs/Rod Stewart - Young Turks (Official HD Remaster) - Rod Stewart.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Rod Stewart - Young Turks (Official HD Remaster) - Rod Stewart.jpg
description:
The official HD remaster of “Young Turks” by Rod Stewart, from his 1981 album Tonight I’m Yours. A high-energy anthem of youth and freedom, Young Turks blends rock, pop, and new wave influences, showcasing Rod Stewart’s ability to evolve with the times.

Released as a single in 1981, the track became a major international hit, reaching the Top 5 in the U.S. and charting strongly in the U.K. The official video captures the spirit of early 80s culture and remains one of Rod Stewart’s most celebrated videos.

🔔 Subscribe to the Rod Stewart channel and turn up the volume on Sir Rod’s greatest hits:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCm09uYWfEygr5gWjDm9xmUA?sub_confirmation=1

🌎 Tour Dates https://www.rodstewart.com/events/

📣 Facebook /rodstewart
📸 Instagram /sirrodstewart
📱 Twitter /rodstewart

The Rod Stewart channel is the official YouTube home for Sir Rod Stewart, the legendary Rock ’n’ Roll Hall of Fame icon with over five decades of chart-topping music. With his unmistakable voice, Rod Stewart has sold more than 200 million records worldwide, earning nine #1 albums, 31 Top 10 singles in the U.K., and 16 Top 10 albums and 26 Top 10 singles in the U.S.

Here you’ll find greatest hits like Maggie May, Tonight’s The Night (Gonna Be Alright), Sailing, I Don’t Want To Talk About It, Stay With Me, Forever Young, The First Cut Is the Deepest, Rhythm of My Heart, and Do Ya Think I’m Sexy?. Enjoy official music videos, legendary live performances including his unforgettable Glastonbury concert, and rare footage from one of the most celebrated careers in music history.

title: Xtrullor - Event Horizon
file_path: Songs/Xtrullor - Event Horizon - Xtrullor.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Xtrullor - Event Horizon - Xtrullor.jpg
description:
DOWNLOAD (From my archive):
https://e.pcloud.link/publink/show?code=kZY7S5Zt9A9w3wmDWjudGJBBHlSbHfpE807

Welcome to the edge of event horizon. There's no turning back now.

An epic orchestral dubstep soundtrack, in D# minor.

=======

I think I'm finally starting to get the hang of this hybrid genre. I had a lot of fun producing this track and I'm actually quite satisfied how it turned out in the end. With this track, I've now probably set myself new standards for composition, mixing and mastering.

Many sleepless nights were spent making this track, but many revelations about composition, sound design, mixing and mastering were also had.

It took about 58 hours to make this track.

Here's a screenshot of the project in FL: https://imgur.com/pwZk2UT

And as usual, enjoy the listening experience!

title: Geometry Dash - Low Death Verified
file_path: Songs/Geometry Dash - Low Death Verified - KrmaL.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Geometry Dash - Low Death Verified - KrmaL.jpg
description:
Sometime in late 2015, for one reason or another, I got Migueword's go-ahead to copy and buff his fantastic 9-star level High Life, resulting in a level that would later be named Low Death by the suggestion of one of his friends. This was around the time when Conical Depression was being built, and Rearmed (still only known as Unarmed) was set to be decorated by him—in other words, before I had any confidence in my ability to create a good level myself. It's riddled with awful, outdated memes, intentionally frustrating gameplay, and various secrets and in-jokes, as that's essentially all this level was ever meant to be: an in-joke. The sheer difficulty of this level at the time, though, combined with the relatively low profile High Life had at the moment, led a lot to believe this was my own originally-conceived level, and much confusion ensued over time. By the time we were far past the joke and I was on to work on more serious projects with miles more effort than this one, a lot of people still wanted to see this level completed and released, despite myself on multiple occasions expressing that I wanted nothing to do with it.

In late February of 2017, my Geometry Dash account, among many others, was breached, and my password was leaked to the public, giving anyone and everyone access to my account, and that led to some pretty unfortunate events: Most, if not all of my demon levels had secret ways added to them, the original (invisible) M A N I X was deleted, and various unfinished levels, including Black Blizzard and Low Death, were uploaded to my account. I got in contact with RobTop to sort the first couple things out, but there wasn't much he could do about the last problem. He tried to soften the blow by deleting copies of the leaks I'd send him, but by that point a lot of people already had them saved and ready to put right back up, so it was a fairly pointless and hopeless battle to fight. The best we could do was hope people wouldn't abuse it. Ha...

Over time you'd often see me mock or tease those who still wanted to see this level out, because I had come to accept that I'd never get it off my back. Various video gags, me posting a status saying I deleted the level, and plenty of outside drama I'm sure at least some of you are familiar with, all culminated in what was probably a total mess of confusion on this level for anyone that hadn't been following my channel for that long. In secret, there have been many times where I'd come back to trying to beat the level, just in case there was a chance or reason it'd happen. Come January of this year, a lot of the hype had finally died down, and I found the time and motivation to jump back into it, with the motivation of an actual deadline: April 1st. I figured there's no better day to release a level that serves only as a joke; an infamous one. Under the pseudonym "Gangnam Style," I gave updates to Migueword among other friends of mine to keep the internal excitement up, and on March 5th, 2020, at 11:30 EST, what you see in this video happened.

As for whether this level should be rated: I do not know. Ultimately it's just a buffed and destroyed copy of someone else's level, and its difficulty doesn't really mean much today either, but there's undoubtedly a lot of history and excitement and behind it either way. During a 2016 stream, I recall at some point Rob said he'd rate it if it came out, but that was so long ago, it's no big deal if he doesn't—standards and expectations are higher now. Either way, I hope those of you who were somehow still waiting for this will get all the enjoyment out of it that you can; I'm certainly happy to be done with it. And since I've been asked a lot, yes, this is a legitimate completion; the April Fools' joke was just premiering the video with the title and thumbnail reading "Gangnam Style."

Music: Romos - Magic Touch (31 song mashup)

title: Shane McMahon - Here Comes The Money (Entrance Theme)
file_path: Songs/Shane McMahon - Here Comes The Money (Entrance Theme) - WWEMusic.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Shane McMahon - Here Comes The Money (Entrance Theme) - WWEMusic.jpg
description:
Shane McMahon - Here Comes The Money (Entrance Theme)

Lyrics

Here comes the money, here we go, money 
Here comes the money 
Money, money, money, money, money, money, money, money, money. 

Dolla dolla, dolla dolla 

Ching ching, bling bling, cut the chatter 
If you ain’t talkin’ money then your talking don’t matter 
Ching ching, bling bling, pattin’ pockets 
You take the dolla dolla, can’t a damn soul stop it 

Shockin’, here comes the new kid on the block, hold all your bets, 
Here’s where the buck stops. See, first of all I’m steppin’ out on my own. 
‘Bout time I elevated to claim my own throne.  
Success in my blood, call it homegrown. Pores reeking testosterone. 
Power and money got me crazy cocky, no longer need you Poppy. 
I know your mad at me, cause you can’t stop me. 
And if you wonder how this player done scooped your honey, 
I think she smelled my cologne, it’s called ‘Brand New Money’. 
Making major moves, man...ain’t a damn thing funny. 
Pimpin’ hood rats to Playboy Bunnies, they see the... 

Money, money, money, money, money, money, money, money, money. 
Money, money, money, money, money, money, money, money, money. 

I glow with dollar dollar, and roll about fifty, 
Like to go wild smelling fresh and looking spiffy 
Don’t like clean money, I want my wrist to be filthy 
Plus with a retirement fund I can’t touch till I’m sixty 
So what am I supposed to do, rollin’ through, 
Steady padding in the pocket, sounds like a hole in two 
Ching ching, bling bling, catching cash in lumps, 
It’s a cold world, get a jacket, I’m selling ‘em out my trunk. 
Whenever whatever it takes to shake down a dollar 
More dough in that direction, wait a minute, holler holler. 
All wants to know where they go, where they went 
In a mink and march on money, smellin’ just like a mint. 

Now if you can’t see the money, get your eyes cleaned with Visine 
I need fine things with seven digits at a time team. 
My cheerin’ chillin, the best, never worse, see we never get the pebbles... 
They’ve got the rocks first. Bank vaults are locking, ching ching we’re mocking’ 
I’m rocking’, my family’s Christmas stockings are shocking. 
Fine women, any weather, naughty denim and leather 
Call it tricking’ or whatever, son they get better. 
I’m one smart cookie, the vet that smash rookies. 
My stocks on top, your checks bounce while mine boogie. 
Wrist, we rock it, chicks stop it or knock it 
Cash looks like I got a gang of fists in my pockets.

title: Rick Astley - Never Gonna Give You Up (Official Video) (4K Remaster)
file_path: Songs/Rick Astley - Never Gonna Give You Up (Official Video) (4K Remaster) - Rick Astley.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Rick Astley - Never Gonna Give You Up (Official Video) (4K Remaster) - Rick Astley.jpg
description:
The official video for “Never Gonna Give You Up” by Rick Astley. 

Never: The Autobiography 📚 OUT NOW! 
Follow this link to get your copy and listen to Rick’s ‘Never’ playlist ❤️ #RickAstleyNever
https://linktr.ee/rickastleynever

“Never Gonna Give You Up” was a global smash on its release in July 1987, topping the charts in 25 countries including Rick’s native UK and the US Billboard Hot 100.  It also won the Brit Award for Best single in 1988. Stock Aitken and Waterman wrote and produced the track which was the lead-off single and lead track from Rick’s debut LP “Whenever You Need Somebody”.  The album was itself a UK number one and would go on to sell over 15 million copies worldwide.

The legendary video was directed by Simon West – who later went on to make Hollywood blockbusters such as Con Air, Lara Croft – Tomb Raider and The Expendables 2.  The video passed the 1bn YouTube views milestone on 28 July 2021.

Subscribe to the official Rick Astley YouTube channel: https://RickAstley.lnk.to/YTSubID

title: Exyl & Dalux - Nice logo
file_path: Songs/Exyl & Dalux - Nice logo - Exyl.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Exyl & Dalux - Nice logo - Exyl.jpg
description:
Listen everywhere: https://ffm.to/exyl-nice-logo
Let's connect: https://ffm.bio/exyl

As for the logos... Me and Dalux have very similar logos by pure coincidence... I guess we both had the same idea with the letter X lol. The main distinguishing feature is the fact we put gaps in different spots on the logo. My logo was meant to be an interesting way to write the letters e, x, y, and l... while Dalux says his logo represents the letters D, A, and X... There are allegations that Dalux is somehow my alt account, which would be funny, but... No. Dalux is a Norwegian guy and I am an Egyptian-Canadian guy. 

Me and Dalux became friends when I randomly joined his discord server WAY back in the day... I was talking to someone in the old Trap Nation server and he joked that my logo looks like Dalux, so I found his stuff and had a funny discussion with him back in 2018, way before I uploaded anything. Turns out he was a super talented musician & video editor in his own right and we got along amazingly.

Dalux commented "nice logo" under the song Ping... which I found funny, so I included it in the subtitles of Ping... Those legendary subtitles helped make that song go even more viral. Later, me and Dalux decided to collaborate on this song for april fools of 2020 last second. The music video is really abstract and meme-y because I didn't give myself much time LOL... I created the entire song and video within the last 2 weeks before the deadline we gave ourselves (for April fools)... But the result was legendary within the community hehe.

Dalux's channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UChZS1_df2_r3e-xcditM8Sg

...

Epilepsy warning. 
Song ID for GD creators: 923513

title: Mission Impossible Theme (Full Theme)
file_path: Songs/Mission Impossible Theme (Full Theme) - WALES2468 COLLECTIVE.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Mission Impossible Theme (Full Theme) - WALES2468 COLLECTIVE.jpg
description:
In 1996, Adam Clayton and Larry Mullen Jr. remade the Mission Impossible theme track, creating two new versions of the song: the main one, found here, and another variation called "Mission Accomplished." The main version played during the film's end credits. 

Unlike the original, most of this version follows a regular beat, except for the beginning. The instrumental version became popular worldwide, reaching number seven on the USA Billboard Hot 100 chart, ultimately receiving a Gold certification. It also reached number one in Finland, Hungary, and Iceland, number two in Australia and Ireland, and number seven in the United Kingdom.

Mission Impossible Theme Credits: 

Music written by Lalo Schifrin
Performed by Larry Mullen and Adam Clayton
String arrangement by Craig Armstrong
Percussion by Bashiri Johnson
Produced and arranged by Larry Mullen with David Beal
Engineered by David Beal and Dan Gellert
Mixed by Mark "Spike" Stent
Assisted by Adam Brown
Excerpt from the original TV "Mission: Impossible" theme conducted by Lalo Schifrin
"This tape will self destruct..." dialogue spoken by Henry Czerny
Remaining dialogue spoken by Junior Vasquez

description:
This is the full version of the song played during the warthog run in the final level of Halo 3.  This song has a very pronounced drum track that makes this version of the Halo theme unique, and it is not available on any soundtracks in its entirety.

I noticed the only available versions of this track have the background ambient sounds, which is extremely annoying.  This is the original, halo3_trench (played during the "trench run").

title: Ride of the Valkyries - TrackMania
file_path: Songs/Ride of the Valkyries - TrackMania - DoodleChaos.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Ride of the Valkyries - TrackMania - DoodleChaos.jpg
description:
I created a race track matching "Ride of the Valkyries" by Richard Wagner using TrackMania's level editor.

When I learned TrackMania had a deterministic physics engine I instantly wanted to try this. Turns out 3-D music synchronization is more complicated than 2-D projects I've done in the past with Line Rider. 

Although there are checkpoints in TrackMania, there is no way to save the velocity, angles, and position of the vehicle midway through the track that I know of. Therefore, I had to restart from the beginning every time I wanted to adjust or move a block. I think I hold the record for most times listening to this piece in a month. 

If you would like to support me making videos for this channel here is the link to my Patreon page: https://www.patreon.com/doodlechaos

Contact:
https://www.instagram.com/me.robbins/
https://twitter.com/doodlechaos
contact@doodlechaos.com

title: Audio Crime - Atomic Amnesia
file_path: Songs/Audio Crime - Atomic Amnesia - kliksphilip.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Audio Crime - Atomic Amnesia - kliksphilip.jpg
description:
Audio Crime is my second album and consists of my 12th song right through to my 20th. They were all made between November 2008 and June 2009 and each has had weeks of tweaking.

Track 1 - Nursery Nightmare
Track 2 - Superhero
Track 3 - Jimmy's War
Track 4 - That's Life
Track 5 - Underpass
Track 6 - Atomic Amnesia
Track 7 - Ticking Clock
Track 8 - Ancients
Track 9 - Heart Attack

DOWNLOAD THE ALBUM HERE

http://tinyurl.com/ACalbum

Atomic Amnesia
(17th November 2008 - 14th February 2009)
This song was started even before Crevasse was, meaning that this should have been on my first album. However, it wasn't before its length and quality surpassed the others on my first album, so I pushed this one forwards to Audio Crime just to make sure I had at least one that was up to the standards set by my first album (Hey, I hadn't made any of the other songs at that time, my music making journey was unclear!). I very nearly left this as a very simple tune, but after I discovered that I could add more bits to the later sequences the song suddenly became far more interesting. The 'first' final version was 5 and a half minutes long- I cut it down a bit to try and make it a bit less tedious and closer in length to the other songs on this album. This song reminds me of my first term at University and I played this one again and again as I walked across campus, thinking up ways of improving it.

FOR MORE MUSIC GO TO

http://www.3kliksphilip.com

title: Hans Zimmer - No Time For Caution (Interstellar Soundtrack)(Docking)(Interstellar OST)
file_path: Songs/Hans Zimmer - No Time For Caution (Interstellar Soundtrack)(Docking)(Interstellar OST) - 711TAC.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Hans Zimmer - No Time For Caution (Interstellar Soundtrack)(Docking)(Interstellar OST) - 711TAC.jpg
description:
Interstellar Docking Scene Music - No Time For Caution - Hans Zimmer

Interstellar is a 2014 science fiction film directed by Christopher Nolan. Starring Matthew McConaughey, Anne Hathaway, Jessica Chastain, and Michael Caine, the film features a team of space travelers who travel through a wormhole in search of a new habitable planet. It was written by Jonathan Nolan and Christopher Nolan, Christopher combined his idea with an existing script by his brother that was developed in 2007 for Paramount Pictures and producer Lynda Obst. Nolan is producing the film with Obst and Emma Thomas. Theoretical physicist Kip Thorne, whose works inspired the film, acted as both an executive producer and a scientific consultant for the film.

Warner Bros., who produced and distributed some of Nolan's previous films, negotiated with Paramount, traditionally a rival studio, to have a financial stake in Interstellar. Legendary Pictures, which formerly partnered with Warner Bros., also sought a stake. The three companies co-financed the film, and the production companies Syncopy and Lynda Obst Productions were enlisted. The director also hired cinematographer Hoyte van Hoytema since his long-time collaborator Wally Pfister was busy working on Transcendence, his directorial debut. Interstellar was filmed with a combination of anamorphic 35 mm and IMAX 70 mm film photography. Filming took place in the last quarter of 2013 in locations in the province of Alberta, Canada, in southern Iceland, and in Los Angeles, California. The visual effects company Double Negative created visual effects for Interstellar.

Interstellar premiered on October 26, 2014 in Los Angeles, California. Commercially, it is scheduled for a limited release in North America (United States and Canada) on November 5, 2014 and a wide release on November 7, 2014. It will also be released in Belgium, France and Switzerland on November 5, 2014 and in additional territories in the following days, including the United Kingdom on November 7, 2014. For the limited release in North America, it will be released in 70 mm and 35 mm film formats in approximately 240 theaters which still project the formats, including at least 41 70 mm IMAX theaters. For the wide release, it will expand to theaters that will show it in digital format. Paramount Pictures will distribute the film in North America, and Warner Bros. will distribute it in the remaining territories

Interstellar OST - No Time For Caution - Hans Zimmer (Original Motion Picture Soundtrack)


Interstellar Docking Soundtrack

title: Doom OST - E1M1 - At Doom's Gate
file_path: Songs/Doom OST - E1M1 - At Doom's Gate - Jim DarkMagic.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Doom OST - E1M1 - At Doom's Gate - Jim DarkMagic.jpg
description:
E1M1
http://www.bpmusic.com

Bobby Prince: "The id Software development team originally wanted me to do nothing but metal songs for DOOM. I did not think that this type of music would be appropriate throughout the game, but I roughed out several original songs and also created MIDI sequences of some cover material. This was before any level design and was before most of the artwork had been created. As the game came together, the guys at id saw that this type of music was not appropriate for many of the levels in DOOM. Thinking that this would be the case, I had also roughed out a lot of ambient moody background music, much of which ended up in the game. This song was one of the first of its type that I wrote. I heard it as being on a level that went by real fast. As it turns out, John Romero (who placed all of the songs on the levels) decided it was a perfect song for the first level".

Synopsis:
Choices... to choose between right or wrong is simple, but what defines one's life is the decision between the greater of two goods or the lesser of two evils. This is the advice that Zooni Ali Beg (Kajol) receives from her father just as she is about to venture out into the world of her own for the very first time. Little does she know that these very words will shape her life.

Zooni, a blind Kashmiri girl, meets Rehan Qadri (Aamir Khan), a local Delhi tour guide and an incorrigible flirt. Her friends warn her against this good-for-nothing roadside Romeo, but she chooses to ignore them. It is now her time to discover life and love. Is this really the right choice?

Rehan is fascinated by Zooni and wants her to see life as it should be seen, in its many colours. He promises her that the time spent with him will be the most precious in all her life. Zooni sees Delhi, life and love like she never has before, because of Rehan.

What Zooni doesn't know is that Rehan has another side of his life that he has kept from her - something that can not only change her life, but can also destroy it.

Synopsis:
The tale begins in Mumbai where a sophisticated gang of robbers is sweeping through the city, giving nightmares to the police department. They come like wind, sweep the place and disappear on their hi-tech bikes - the slickest and fastest riding machines on the road.

Officer Jai Dixit (Abhishek Bachchan) is brought in on the case. Soon he is on the trail of the robbers - a gang headed by Kabir (John Abraham), an evil perfectionist with an attitude. Jai ropes in the services of Ali (Uday Chopra), a happy-go-lucky garage mechanic and a prodigious bike rider. The two reluctant partners join forces, and soon Jai realizes that the clown can ride bikes like the wind. Kabir soon catches on to Jai-Ali’s teaming up and his arrogance pushes him to take up the challenge openly. From the mean streets of Mumbai, the chase shifts to the sun kissed beaches of Goa. And so starts the hunt... where sometimes the hunter becomes the hunted.

Dhoom reinvents the classic cops and robbers tale and brings it into the 21st century. With fast bikes, big action, non-stop fun and a thrilling story that leaves you out of breath, Dhoom is a powerhouse of adventure and excitement waiting to explode. It’s like your favourite roller coaster ride... once you’re on, the only thing you want to do is to go back and take that ride all over again!

Synopsis:
The world’s oldest desert, the Namib - A solitary railway line... A royal family on a royal journey. From the sky... like lightening... A flash... the centuries old royal crown is stolen.

Back in action... DHOOM:2 reinvents the action comedy genre and propels it into the 21st century. Ali’s (Uday Chopra) dream of becoming a police officer has come true. He is now ACP Jai Dixit’s (Abhishek Bachchan) right hand man. Together they are trying to keep a tight leash on the crime in India, little do they know what they are going to be up against.

Enter Aryan - Mr.A (Hrithik Roshan) A high tech international thief after pulling off a series of impossible heists all over the world. His next target is Mumbai, India. The case is given to ACP Jai Dixit and Ali helping them put the pieces of the puzzle together is ACP Shonali Bose (Bipasha Basu). For the last two years Shonali has been tracking these amazing thefts and is now an expert on this thief, who no one has seen.

Once in Mumbai Mr.A finds his match in Sunehri (Aishwarya Rai). A petty yet clever thief. She makes him an offer he finds very hard to refuse. A partnership Aryan accepts. And so the game begins.

The cops, Jai, Shonali and Ali and they are after Aryan and Sunehri - The Robbers.

From the desert of Namibia to the backwaters of Goa, the mean streets of Mumbai and the ancient forts of Rajasthan and finally to wild and exotic Rio Brazil. Does ACP Jai Dixit manage to nab Mr.A?

Go on! Enjoy the ride!! Once again!!!

https://goo.gl/6bYRSp

《陽光彩虹小白馬》是大張偉最新專輯《人間精品》中的一首單曲，已於4月16日上線，歡快的旋律搭配暖心的歌詞頗受好評。大張偉用自己燥而不亂的朋克方式闡述暖心積極的生活態度：任何時候請相信“一切都會好噠”。而這首新歌的MV也於今日正式發佈，以手指舞搭配輕鬆歡快的歌曲氛圍，點燃這個春天的最後一把燥火！
據悉，《陽光彩虹小白馬》的創作靈感來自大張偉在《跟著貝爾去旅行》中的一次錄製，當時艱苦的錄影環境和內容讓大張偉瀕臨奔潰，而就在這樣的境況下他能想到最美好的事物居然就是“陽光彩虹小白馬”。除此之外，高壓的工作環境時常讓大張偉感到焦慮和無助，後來他看到一個電影的截屏，深受觸動。上面說道：“心很脆弱，需要你哄它，要輕拍著它，對它說，一切都會好的。”從此以後，大張偉便時常在睡覺前，拍打著心口平靜自己，對它說，一切會好的。於是歌詞就來了，面對一切的鬱悶痛楚焦躁，要記得！你是那個陽光彩虹小白馬，一切會好噠！
或許正是因為這些最真實，最真切的感受，才讓歌曲一經發佈，便引發了廣泛共鳴。很多網友聽後都紛紛表示：“太治癒系了！被歌曲中的快樂力量感染了。”“這首歌讓我想到了自己，這麼歡快的歌曲竟然聽哭了。”確實，在社會焦慮普通存在的當下，生活或許不易，世界或許殘酷，我們或許無助，但仍要告訴自己“你是最強噠最棒噠最亮噠最發光噠”！

About CJ:
Born, Chris Soriano, CJ is a Staten Island Native, but with a Puerto Rican background who began his musical career in 2017 at the age of 20 years old. His musical influences include hip hop royalty such as Wu Tang Clan, Jay Z, 50 Cent and Pop Smoke. Raised on the sidelines of the music business, his explosive delivery, intertwined with floe-tic bars, have always been an ear catcher to fans as well as industry executives. 

While independently pushing his talents and personally structuring his core team, CJ has worked with producers like Dizzy Banko, who produced his song “Pop”.

On August 20, 2020, CJ released “WHOOPTY" his critically acclaimed single independently with Equity distribution, which gained over 1 million views on YouTube and million on Spotify after a month of its debut. That along with over 4m fan Tik Tok videos have driven the awareness of CJ’s debut. Song delivery mixed with his explosive music video, CJ is definitely one to watch and not be taken lightly.

,And don't forget the joker!

The only thing you see, you know it's gonna be,

The Ace Of Spades

**About Finger Eleven**
Finger Eleven is a Canadian rock band from Burlington, Ontario, formed in 1990. They have released seven studio albums (six as Finger Eleven and one as Rainbow Butt Monkeys), with their album The Greyest of Blue Skies bringing them into the mainstream. Their 2003 self-titled album achieved Gold status in the United States and Platinum in Canada, largely from the success of the single "One Thing", which marked the band's first placing on the US Hot 100 Chart at number 16. Their 2007 album, Them vs. You vs. Me launched the single "Paralyzer", which went on to top the Canadian Hot 100 and both US rock charts, as well as reaching No. 6 on the US Hot 100 and No. 12 on the Australian Singles Chart. They won the Juno Award for Rock Album of the Year in 2008.[5] The same album was later certified gold in the US and multi-platinum in Canada. They released their sixth studio album, Life Turns Electric, on October 5, 2010; it was nominated as Best Rock Album of the Year at the Juno Awards of 2011. Five Crooked Lines, their 7th studio album, was released in 2015. Between 1995 and 2016, Finger Eleven was among the top 75 best-selling Canadian artists in Canada and among the top 25 best-selling Canadian bands in Canada.

India's most prominent bass music producer NUCLEYA released a brand new EP 'Bass Rani' on September 27th 2015. One of the early proponents of the now-popular genre of Dubstep in India, NUCLEYA has built on that start to emerge as one of the most exciting, experimental and engaging producers in the Indian scene in the past seven years. He has made his mark as an Indian dance music producer, focussing on sounds and textures that are Indian in sound but international in appeal. His new EP 'Bass Rani' explores global BASS and cements NUCLEYA's position as the Indian leader in the 'global bass' movement, as he continues to look to the future for new sounds.

title: The Godfather Theme (Jaydon Lewis Trap Remix)
file_path: Songs/The Godfather Theme (Jaydon Lewis Trap Remix) - Trap City.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/The Godfather Theme (Jaydon Lewis Trap Remix) - Trap City.jpg
description:
A Jaydon Lewis Trap tribute to one of the greatest movie masterpieces of all-time, The Godfather.
 
For all you gangster, mafia, and Italian mob movie fans out there. Today we present this dope Trap remix by none other than Jaydon Lewis. Now I know it might sound controversial to remix such a beautiful classical theme song from The Godfather into a bass-fueled journey. Jaydon Lewis made sure to stay true to the original as much as possible and just focussed on capturing the soul and element from the original theme song. Driving on top of the emotion that it evokes and further amplifying the feelings by adding a Trap element underneath the song. Inspired by the New York setting where the original The Godfather movie plays in, we’ve selected some beautiful footage from the “City that never sleeps”.

title: Harry Belafonte - Banana Boat Song (Day-O)
file_path: Songs/Harry Belafonte - Banana Boat Song (Day-O) - Ti Ger.webm
thumbnail_path: Songs/thumbnails/Harry Belafonte - Banana Boat Song (Day-O) - Ti Ger.jpg
description:
Der Banana Boat Song („Bananenboot-Lied") ist ein jamaikanisches Calypso-Volkslied. Der Song geht zurück auf ein altes jamaikanisches Volkslied mit dem Titel "Day dah light", das von Schauerleuten handelt, die in Nachtschicht Bananen verluden und über ihre Arbeit sangen. 
Sie erlebten den Tagesanbruch und das damit einhergehende Schichtende: "Daylight come and me wanna go home". Dieser Refrain ist ebenso wie die Melodie allen frühen, traditionellen Versionen des Liedes gemeinsam. Die Strophen variierten jedoch und wurden oft wahrscheinlich aus dem Stegreif erfunden.

-------------------------------------------------------------------

description:
What is up guys! Cobra here with a new song ready for listening!

I am a huge Monstercat fan and have decided to use their Audio Visual to let YOU guys see what your favorite songs look like with it! All you have to do is leave a comment, and I will look at the song, get it, and then put it into a Monstercat visual!
You can say ANY song! I don't care what genre it is, either!

*All credit for the original visualizer goes to Monstercat, as I am just a fan that really knows how to use Adobe After Effects.
**All songs are not mine, AT ALL. This doesn't really matter for I do not monetize my videos, and so there should not be any copyright issues, especially since I put the artist and song name as the title.
***If I ever start using pictures in my videos (again, for those of you who have seen my past vids), I will put a link to where I got it from, and most likely the name of it or (since I like to use Anime) show/manga it is from.

I hope you guys enjoy this as much as I love to see my finished products! Thank you for watching.
